design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate procedurally-hazardous stages while managing limited ammunition and positioning for optimal coverage against dense enemy waves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Ammunition scarcity balanced against screen saturation—player must choose between aggressive suppression and conservative resource preservation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mercenary/elite soldier executing precision military operations with tactical weapon selection across diverse combat theaters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Gunforce II commits to ammunition-limited design in a genre typically focused on bullet-dodging; this inverts typical shmup resource tension from 'avoid projectiles' to 'manage firepower'. Stage variety and weapon switching are core identity rather than novelty.

difficulty.lives_system: Limited continues with stage-based checkpoint resurrection
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present; limited attempts per run encourage stage mastery
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoints; progression loss on final stage defeat is punitive
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Weapon selection and positioning strategy provide tactical agency; ammunition rationing forces meaningful tactical choices but removes direct difficulty adjustment

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system with multiple firearm types (grenades, rapid-fire, spread patterns, heavy ordnance); weapons are temporary pickups rather than permanent upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression unlocks subsequent linear stages; weapon availability determined by enemy drops and environmental spawns
replayability_hooks: Stage-specific enemy patterns encourage learning and speedrun potential

world_building_techniques: Stage theming implies mercenary deployment across global conflict zones (industrial, desert, urban, tropical settings)
Minimal briefing text establishes mission context
Enemy variety and architecture suggest distinct theaters of operation

boss_design.philosophy: Punctuation encounters that demand accumulated ammunition reserves and pattern recognition; bosses test resource management through attrition

faction_indicators: Mercenary protagonist implied through mission variety and neutral global deployment
Enemy types suggest state actors and irregular forces across multiple theaters
danger_signals: Ammunition scarcity creates high-stakes resource decisions
Boss encounters punish poor ammunition conservation decisions made earlier in stage
environmental_storytelling: Stage architecture (military installations, urban warfare zones, natural terrain) conveys deployment diversity
Enemy unit composition reflects regional conflict contexts
